items.what_it_depicts: Car and Driver photograph from Csaba Csere's November 1987 archived test of the Porsche 959.
items.why_add: Period magazine editorial photograph from the exhibit's primary written source.
items.true_original_source: Car and Driver, November 1987, Csaba Csere 959 archived test
items.chicago_citation: Car and Driver, Porsche 959 Road Test Photograph, November 1987, via Hearst archives.

items.what_it_depicts: Studio photograph of the 959 accompanying Csere's archived Car and Driver test.
items.why_add: Second editorial photograph from the primary written source.
items.chicago_citation: Car and Driver, Porsche 959 Studio Photograph, 1987, via Hearst.

items.what_it_depicts: David Kimble's factory-style cutaway illustration of the Porsche 959 published by Motorsport.com.
items.why_add: Highest-resolution cutaway directly from the secondary source the exhibit cites; engineering-story anchor.
items.chicago_citation: David Kimble, Porsche 959 Cutaway Illustration, Motorsport.com, June 8, 2016.

items.what_it_depicts: Porsche's factory press photograph of the 'Gruppe B' concept car at the 1983 Frankfurt IAA - the 959's official reveal.
items.why_add: Period factory press image of the Frankfurt 1983 reveal; fills the IAA debut gap.
items.true_original_source: Porsche AG press archive via StuttCars
items.chicago_citation: Porsche AG, Porsche 'Gruppe B' Concept at 1983 Frankfurt IAA, 1983, press archive via StuttCars.

items.what_it_depicts: Factory image showing the 1983 Gruppe B concept in Rothmans-presentation livery.
items.why_add: Documents the Frankfurt-to-Paris-Dakar development thread visually.
items.true_original_source: Porsche AG press archive via StuttCars
items.chicago_citation: Porsche AG, Porsche 959 'Gruppe B' Concept, Rothmans Livery, 1983, via StuttCars.

items.what_it_depicts: The Group B 959 prototype now in the Prototyp Museum, Hamburg.
items.why_add: Museum specimen of the preserved Frankfurt 1983 prototype.
items.chicago_citation: Ad Meskens, Porsche 959 Prototype Group B at Hamburg Prototyp Museum, 2024, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The Rothmans-liveried 959 that won the 1986 Paris-Dakar Rally (Metge/Lemoyne #185), Porsche Museum.
items.why_add: The motorsport crown of the 959's development story; Csere cited its rally pedigree in 1987.
items.chicago_citation: Valder137, Porsche 959 1986 Paris-Dakar Rothmans Racer, Porsche Museum, 2013, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: 1985 Porsche 959 Paris-Dakar prototype — the Ickx/Brasseur entry that preceded the 1986 winner.
items.why_add: Shows the 1984-1986 evolution of the rally 959 development program.
items.chicago_citation: edvvc, 1985 Porsche 959 Paris-Dakar, Flickr / Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The Porsche 961, a 959-based Le Mans race car, in its 1987 Le Mans No. 203 livery.
items.why_add: Completes the motorsport thread: 959 → 961 on the racetrack.
items.chicago_citation: Alexander Migl, Porsche 961 (1987 Le Mans No. 203), Porsche Museum, 2020, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0.

items.what_it_depicts: Factory mechanical sketch of the 959 on display at the Porsche Museum Stuttgart.
items.why_add: Period technical illustration of the PSK AWD system, twin-sequential turbos; engineering centerpiece.
items.chicago_citation: Rouk40130, Porsche 959 Mechanical Sketch, Porsche Museum Stuttgart, 2024, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 4.0.

items.what_it_depicts: Ralph Lauren's 1988 Porsche 959 Komfort — the private American owner who, with Gates and Allen, pushed for 959 import legalization.
items.why_add: Ties to the Gates import saga; Lauren's 959 is a documented cultural artifact.
items.chicago_citation: Uncredited, 1988 Porsche 959 Komfort, Ralph Lauren Collection, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The Porsche 959's twin-sequential-turbo 2.85L flat-six engine, open-engine-bay view.
items.why_add: Shows the engine Csere praised for 'perfect' throttle response; pairs with the cutaway.
items.chicago_citation: Sfoskett, Porsche 959 Engine, 2005, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0.
